articles.title: Fresh plea claims existence of Jain temple, gurukul at disputed Bhojshala-Kamal Maula mosque site
articles.description: The court has granted two weeks time to the government authorities to file their objections after they raised questions at the maintainability of the plea as a public interest litigation (PIL). The matter has been listed for April 2

topStories.title: Trump Says He Doesn't Want Ceasefire In Iran
topStories.description: US President Donald Trump on Friday ruled out reaching a ceasefire agreement with Iran, saying Washington has the upper hand in the three-week-old war.

topStories.title: How old is Barron Trump? What we know about US President Donald Trump's youngest son?
topStories.description: As Barron Trump turns 20, he is increasingly focused on business and education. He maintains a low profile while attending New York University, with much of his early life kept private, highlighting his preference for a quieter path away from media attention.

topStories.title: ‘Enemy defeated…’: Mojtaba Khamenei says Iran dealt enemies ‘dizzying blow’ as conflict with US, Israel drags on
topStories.description: Mojtaba Khamenei further claimed that Israel and the US had the ‘illusion’ that if Ali Khamenei and other military leaders were killed, they would create ‘fear and despair’ and ‘realise the dream of dominating Iran and then dividing it’

topStories.title: Congress high command will decide Cabinet reshuffle: Home Minister
topStories.description: G. Parameshwara says it is natural to have aspirations, and there is nothing wrong in seeking a ministerial post.

topStories.title: Bypolls: BJP ahead in candidate selection, Congress weighs options
topStories.description: The bypolls have been necessitated owing to the deaths of incumbent MLAs H.Y. Meti in Bagalkot and Shamanur Shivashankarappa in Davangere; both served as Ministers during Siddaramaiah’s previous tenure as Chief Minister

topStories.title: CAPF Bill says IPS necessary for Union-State coordination, reserves 50% I-G rank posts for IPS
topStories.description: The Bill seeks to negate a May 23, 2025 Supreme Court judgment which asked the Home Ministry to “progressively reduce” IPS deputation in CAPFs up to the rank of I-Gs in the next two years

dailyNews.title: UK Approves US Using Its Bases To Strike Iran Sites Targeting Ships
dailyNews.description: British ministers met on Friday to discuss the Iran war and Iran's blocking of the Strait of Hormuz, according to a Downing Street statement.

dailyNews.title: Just 5 Votes: Rajya Sabha Poll Math Puts Congress On Edge In Madhya Pradesh
dailyNews.description: With three Rajya Sabha seats from the state set to fall vacant and elections expected in April-May, the battle will hinge on numbers.

dailyNews.title: Russian Quid Pro Quo: Moscow Says Won't Share Intel With Iran If US Cuts Off Ukraine
dailyNews.description: The existence of the proposal has raised eyebrows regarding Russia trying to create a rift between America and Europe.

dailyNews.description: The joining programme was held in the presence of Assam BJP President and MP from Darrang-Udalguri, Dilip Saikia.

dailyNews.title: Eid Message: Iran Says It Seeks Peace With Neighbours, Reiterates No Nuclear-Weapon Ambiti
dailyNews.description: President Masoud Pezeshkian calls for regional cooperation and security framework, amid ongoing tensions with the US and Israel.

dailyNews.title: "Hostile Environment": Team Trump Sues Harvard Over Anti-Israeli Protests
dailyNews.description: Harvard has been a central focus of the president's campaign to force changes at major US universities, which Donald Trump has derided for alleged antisemitic and "radical left" ideologies

dailyNews.title: NATO exit from Iraq - Alliance pulls out hundreds of personnel as US' conflict with Iran rages on
dailyNews.description: The personnel include troops from NATO allies, along with partner countries such as Austria and Australia

dailyNews.title: US Education Department transfers student loan management to Treasury Dept — Here's what changes for borrowers
dailyNews.description: The US Education Department has transferred student loans to the Treasury Department, marking a significant step toward the Trump administration's dismantling of the agency. Here's what changes for the borrowers:

dailyNews.title: US accuses Iran’s government of operating hacktivist group that hacked Stryker
dailyNews.description: The U.S. Justice Department said an Iranian security ministry operates the fake activist persona known as Handala, which claimed responsibility for the destructive hack targeting medical tech giant Stryker.

dailyNews.title: Did Chuck Norris suffer heart attack? Fact check on theories swirling around martial arts master’s death
dailyNews.description: The Delta Force and Walker, Texas Ranger star was hospitalised in Hawaii after an unknown incident occurred on the island of Kauai.

dailyNews.title: Trump’s AI framework targets state laws, shifts child safety burden to parents
dailyNews.description: Trump’s AI framework pushes federal preemption of state laws, emphasizes innovation, and shifts responsibility for child safety toward parents while laying out lighter-touch rules for tech companies.

dailyNews.title: Trump administration sues Harvard over treatment of Jewish and Israeli students, seeks to recover billions in funds
dailyNews.description: The Justice Department's lawsuit against Harvard alleges the university is indifferent to antisemitism on campus. The suit aims to enforce compliance and recover taxpayer money, amidst ongoing scrutiny of university practices regarding Jewish and Israeli students.

dailyNews.title: Trump calls NATO cowards for not sending troops to help open Strait of Hormuz
dailyNews.description: President Trump said he is considering "winding down" the military efforts against Iran. However, multiple sources tell CBS News that Pentagon officials are preparing for the possibility of sending U.S. ground forces into Iran. CBS News senior White House correspondent Weijia Jiang.

dailyNews.title: Ret. Gen. Joseph Votel says "it's hard to make that connection" between NATO and Strait of Hormuz
dailyNews.description: NATO countries have so far rejected President Trump's call for help with the Strait of Hormuz. Ret. Gen. Joseph Votel, former commander of U.S. Central Command, joins to discuss.

dailyNews.title: Trump lashes out at NATO for not sending troops to Strait of Hormuz
dailyNews.description: President Trump on Friday called NATO allies "cowards" for declining to send troops to help reopen the Strait of Hormuz. CBS News senior White House correspondent Weijia Jiang has more.

dailyNews.title: Trump calls NATO allies "cowards" as more Marines head toward Middle East
dailyNews.description: The Iran war is nearing the three-week mark as about 2,200 more U.S. Marines and three more warships are headed toward the region, two U.S. officials say.

dailyNews.title: Trump says "we don't need" the Strait of Hormuz as NATO allies decline to help reopen it
dailyNews.description: President Trump on Friday said the U.S. does not need the Strait of Hormuz, through which 20% of the world's oil passes, and said other nations "will have to get involved." CBS News White House reporter Aaron Navarro has more.

dailyNews.title: White House unveils AI framework for Congress
dailyNews.description: The White House unveiled a national framework for how it wants Congress to address concerns about artificial intelligence. Technology journalist Jacob Ward joins CBS News to discuss the outline and AI concerns.

dailyNews.title: Former Iranian prisoners ask Trump to get Americans held there out
dailyNews.description: Former prisoners in Iran, Emad Shargi and Siamak Namazi, told "Face the Nation" moderator Margaret Brennan their message to President Trump about current Americans held there.

dailyNews.title: U.S. Prosecutors Are Investigating Colombia President Gustavo Petro
dailyNews.description: The Justice Department is examining whether President Gustavo Petro of Colombia has ties to drug traffickers.

dailyNews.title: Trump Friend Asked ICE to Detain the Mother of His Child
dailyNews.description: Paolo Zampolli, a former modeling agent and a longtime Trump ally, was in a custody battle over his son. An ICE official agreed to help.

dailyNews.title: Trump weighing several options for U.S. troops in Iran
dailyNews.description: Officials told NBC News that Trump is weighing several options for U.S. ground troops in Iran. There are several options under discussion, the sources said. One would be aimed at freeing up passage in the Strait of Hormuz by deploying troops to Iranian ports or small islands in the Persian Gulf to mitigate the threat to vessels, the sources said.

dailyNews.title: Father of service member killed in Iran war said he never told Pete Hegseth to 'finish' the job
dailyNews.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth and President Donald Trump have said the families of service members who died in the Iran war told them to finish the job.

dailyNews.title: White House expands TrumpRx with three new drugs
dailyNews.description: The White House said Friday it’s adding three drugs for diabetes and lung disease to the self-pay platform TrumpRx, as the administration works to expand the site as part of its push to lower drug prices

dailyNews.title: Trump weighing options for deploying up to thousands of ground troops in Iran
dailyNews.description: WASHINGTON — President Trump is weighing whether to send possibly thousands of U.S. troops into Iran as he looks for a way to achieve some of his key goals and end the war, according to the two current U.S. officials, two former U.S. officials and another person familiar with the discussions
